message:
chore(perf): add #[ignore] to report generators, update stress artifacts

- perf_comparison: add #[ignore] to generate_perf_comparison to keep
  cargo test deterministic (linter applied)
- Updated stress test and perf comparison report artifacts
- Synced bead status updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-150s","title":"Phase 4: Execute Conformance - Official Pi-Mono Extensions (60)","description":"# Phase 4: Execute Conformance - Official Pi-Mono Extensions (60)

## Purpose
Run all 60 official pi-mono example extensions through the differential test runner. These are the MOST important extensions because they define the expected API surface. If any of these fail, we have a runtime bug.

## The 60 Official Extensions (by tier)

### Tier 1 - Minimal (14 extensions)
Single capability, no external deps:
hello, pirate, session-name, custom-footer, custom-header, system-prompt-header, preset, titlebar-spinner, status-line, model-status, widget-placement, question, qna, send-user-message

### Tier 2 - Events Only (11 extensions)
Subscribe to events, no tool registration:
auto-commit-on-exit, bash-spawn-hook, confirm-destructive, dirty-repo-guard, file-trigger, git-checkpoint, input-transform, protected-paths, trigger-compact, timed-confirm, permission-gate

### Tier 3 - Tools (7 extensions)
Register LLM-callable tools:
tools, truncated-tool, tool-override, inline-bash, ssh, interactive-shell, summarize

### Tier 4 - Multi-Capability (12 extensions)
Multiple registrations (tools + events + commands):
bookmark, claude-rules, custom-compaction, event-bus, handoff, notify, todo, mac-system-theme, shutdown-command, dynamic-resources (multi-file), rpc-demo

### Tier 5 - Multi-File (9 extensions)
Require multiple source files and/or npm deps:
doom-overlay, plan-mode, subagent, sandbox, with-deps, custom-provider-anthropic, custom-provider-gitlab-duo, custom-provider-qwen-cli, dynamic-resources

### Tier 6 - UI Heavy (7 extensions)
Complex UI: overlays, editors, games:
overlay-test, overlay-qa-tests, modal-editor, rainbow-editor, message-renderer, snake, space-invaders

## Execution Order
Run tiers in order (1 through 6). Each tier builds confidence before moving to more complex extensions. Early tiers catch fundamental issues; later tiers catch edge cases.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 60 extensions attempted
- 100% pass rate for Tiers 1-3 (32 extensions)
- 95%+ pass rate for Tiers 4-5 (21 extensions)
- 80%+ pass rate for Tier 6 (7 extensions) -- UI extensions may have known differences
- All failures documented with root cause analysis","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:22:13.772092162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:24:42.208949544Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:54:48.963158102Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-150s","depends_on_id":"bd-3odv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:22:13.785715924Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":312,"issue_id":"bd-150s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"EXECUTION STRATEGY: Run tiers in order. Tier 1 is the smoke test -- if hello.ts does not produce identical output in both runtimes, nothing else will work. Fix ALL Tier 1 failures before moving to Tier 2. Each tier exercises progressively more of the API surface, so failures at higher tiers likely indicate specific missing features rather than fundamental bugs.

TIER JUSTIFICATION:
- Tier 1 (14 exts): Basic loading, simple registrations. If these fail, the runtime is broken.
- Tier 2 (11 exts): Event system. Tests pi.on() and handler dispatch.
- Tier 3 (7 exts): Tool system. Tests registerTool() and execution.
- Tier 4 (12 exts): Multiple capabilities. Tests interaction between systems.
- Tier 5 (9 exts): Multi-file + deps. Tests module resolution and import handling.
- Tier 6 (7 exts): UI-heavy. Tests the most complex API surface (overlays, editors, games).","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:27:19Z"},{"id":391,"issue_id":"bd-150s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Ran CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/pi_agent_rust-target cargo test --test ext_conformance_diff. 27 passed, 2 failed. Failures: diff_event_bus -> event_hooks mismatch (TS=[session_start], Rust=[my:notification, session_start]). diff_tool_override -> Rust runtime failed to load: missing export 'appendFileSync' in module 'node:fs'. Full stderr captured in test output.","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:36:56Z"},{"id":394,"issue_id":"bd-150s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Ran: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/pi_agent_rust-target cargo test --test ext_conformance_diff diff_official_manifest. 24 failures. Diff mismatches: bash-spawn-hook + sandbox (bash tool description mismatch + missing timeout param), event-bus (event_hooks mismatch: TS only session_start; Rust includes my:notification), plan-mode (shortcut ctrl+alt+p missing in Rust; extra <unknown> shortcut in Rust). TS oracle load failures (pi-mono node_modules missing exports): custom-compaction, handoff (convertToLlm); custom-header (VERSION); modal-editor, rainbow-editor (CustomEditor); preset, summarize (DynamicBorder); qna (BorderedLoader); subagent (parseFrontmatter); tools (getSettingsListTheme); truncated-tool (formatSize). Rust runtime load failures (missing shims/exports): custom-provider-gitlab-duo (streamSimpleAnthropic in @mariozechner/pi-ai); doom-overlay (parseKey in @mariozechner/pi-tui); interactive-shell (spawnSync in node:child_process); mac-system-theme (node:util unsupported); message-renderer (Box in @mariozechner/pi-tui); overlay-test (CURSOR_MARKER in @mariozechner/pi-tui); space-invaders (isKeyRelease in @mariozechner/pi-tui); ssh (createEditTool in @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent); tool-override (appendFileSync in node:fs).","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:49:16Z"},{"id":398,"issue_id":"bd-150s","author":"TurquoiseCat","text":"Update (TurquoiseCat): made `diff_official_manifest` runnable + fixed TS-oracle package resolution.

- Added progress logging + env filters to `tests/ext_conformance_diff.rs` (`PI_OFFICIAL_FILTER`, `PI_OFFICIAL_MAX`) so the full official manifest run shows per-extension timings.
- Updated TS oracle invocation to run from pi-mono root and prefer pi-mono *workspace* packages (via a per-process `/tmp/.../@mariozechner/*` symlink node-path) instead of the pinned published `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent@0.30.2` in `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/node_modules`.
  - This eliminates the prior TS-oracle load failures for official extensions that require newer exports (e.g. `convertToLlm`, `VERSION`, `CustomEditor`, `DynamicBorder`, etc.).

Current state: `cargo test --test ext_conformance_diff diff_official_manifest -- --ignored --nocapture` now completes and reports **14 failures** (down from 18).

Remaining failure buckets:
1) **Tool-factory shim metadata diffs** (Rust side): `bash-spawn-hook`, `sandbox`, `ssh` — `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` virtual module returns minimal tool defs (missing `timeout` + mismatched descriptions/parameter schemas). Fix by aligning `createBashTool`/`createReadTool`/`createWriteTool`/`createEditTool` metadata to pi-mono.
2) **Missing shim exports** (Rust side):
   - `@mariozechner/pi-ai`: missing `complete`, `streamSimpleOpenAIResponses`.
   - `node:child_process`: missing `exec`.
   - `node:fs`: missing `mkdtempSync`.
   - `@mariozechner/pi-tui`: missing `SettingsList`.
3) **Runtime API gaps / semantics**: `message-renderer` + `preset` fail with `not a function`; `plan-mode` shortcut mismatch.

Notes: I’m blocked from landing the `src/extensions_js.rs` shim tweaks right now due to an active reservation by MagentaCliff, but the failure list above is concrete + reproducible.","created_at":"2026-02-05T10:19:06Z"},{"id":399,"issue_id":"bd-150s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fix applied: Added 'shortcut' field to Rust list_shortcuts() output (src/extensions.rs:7478) to match TS oracle output format. This resolved both remaining failures (plan-mode and preset shortcut mismatches). All 60 official extensions now pass conformance testing. (opus-main-1)","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:23:21Z"},{"id":400,"issue_id":"bd-150s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Update (OpalFlame): ALL 60 official extensions now pass conformance testing!

Key fixes applied to `src/extensions_js.rs`:
1. **registerMessageRenderer API** - Added full implementation:
   - `__pi_message_renderer_index` map for tracking renderers
   - `messageRenderers` field on extension object
   - `__pi_register_message_renderer(customType, renderer)` function
   - Snapshot output includes registered message renderer types
   - Added `registerMessageRenderer` to pi object

2. **Key helper** - Fixed to return strings instead of objects:
   - `Key.ctrlAlt("p")` now returns `"ctrl+alt+p"` (string)
   - Previously returned `{kind: "ctrlAlt", key: "p"}` (object) which caused `<unknown>` in snapshots

3. **Shortcut spec** - Added `shortcut` field to match TS oracle:
   - Shortcut spec now includes: `{ shortcut: keyId, key, key_id, description }`
   - This aligns with TS oracle's expected field name for comparison

Test results:
- `cargo test --test ext_conformance_diff diff_official_manifest -- --ignored` → **60/60 PASS**
- All 16 individual diff tests → **PASS**
- fmt + clippy → **PASS**

Ready to close tier beads and remove `ignore` attribute from `diff_official_manifest` test.","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:23:37Z"},{"id":401,"issue_id":"bd-150s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"MILESTONE ACHIEVED: All 60 official pi-mono extensions now pass differential conformance testing.

Results:
- Tier 1 (14 extensions): 100% pass ✓
- Tier 2 (11 extensions): 100% pass ✓
- Tier 3 (7 extensions): 100% pass ✓
- Tier 4 (12 extensions): 100% pass ✓
- Tier 5 (9 extensions): 100% pass ✓
- Tier 6 (7 extensions): 100% pass ✓

Total: 60/60 (100% pass rate)

Fix applied: Added 'shortcut' field to Rust list_shortcuts() output (commit 03377c8f).

Acceptance criteria met:
✓ All 60 extensions attempted
✓ 100% pass rate for Tiers 1-3 (exceeds 100% requirement)
✓ 100% pass rate for Tiers 4-5 (exceeds 95% requirement)
✓ 100% pass rate for Tier 6 (exceeds 80% requirement)

Next step: Remove #[ignore] attribute from diff_official_manifest test and enable in CI.
(opus-main-1)","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:25:05Z"},{"id":403,"issue_id":"bd-150s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: Fixed all 14 conformance failures! All 60 official pi-mono extensions now pass differential testing.

Fixed shim gaps:
1. @mariozechner/pi-ai: Added complete(), getModel(), streamSimpleOpenAIResponses()
2. @mariozechner/pi-tui: Added DynamicBorder, SettingsList classes
3. node:child_process: Added exec() function
4. node:fs: Added mkdtempSync() function
5. node:os: Added tmpdir() function
6. @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent: Fixed createBashTool, createReadTool, createWriteTool, createEditTool with full descriptions and parameter schemas matching TS runtime
7. Fixed shortcut key-to-string conversion in __pi_register_shortcut()

Test results: cargo test --test ext_conformance_diff diff_official_manifest -- --ignored = PASS (60/60 extensions)","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:31:21Z"},{"id":408,"issue_id":"bd-150s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Update (OpalFlame): Conformance testing complete, ready to close.

Summary of fixes applied this session:
1. **Removed `#[ignore]` from `diff_official_manifest`** - Test now runs in CI
2. **Added retry logic for TS oracle timeouts** - Retries once on timeout (flaky under load)
3. **Fixed clippy warning in `src/http/client.rs`** - `map_or_else` instead of `map().unwrap_or_else()`

Final test results:
- **60/60 official extensions pass** (100%)
- All 32 conformance tests pass (1 ignored community test)
- fmt + clippy pass

Phase 4 acceptance criteria achieved:
- ✅ All 60 extensions attempted
- ✅ 100% pass rate for all tiers (exceeds 80% Tier 6 minimum)
- ✅ No failures to document (TS oracle timeouts are handled by retry)

Ready to close bd-150s and tier sub-beads.","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:54:01Z"},{"id":419,"issue_id":"bd-150s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TS oracle intermittent timeouts observed in test harness. The oracle works correctly when run manually (bun run load_extension.ts), but hangs in the Rust test harness with empty stderr. May be related to process spawning, pipe buffering, or resource contention under load. Retry logic exists but doesn't always help.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:24:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-155","title":"Workstream: extension compatibility documentation + evidence binder complete","description":"Purpose:
- Produce self-contained documentation that maps each sampled extension to evidence of compatibility.

Outputs (artifacts):
- Updated docs (EXTENSIONS.md + FEATURE_PARITY.md).
- Extension Conformance Report (per-extension status table).
- How-to for adding new extensions to the sample + rerunning harness.

Definition of done:
- A reader can understand coverage, gaps, and how to reproduce results without consulting the original plan.
- Docs include links to fixtures and harness commands.

Dependencies:
- Requires sample list, conformance harness results, and benchmarks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] All child/dependent beads include unit tests + E2E scripts with JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9 (or N/A with rationale) and are closed
- [ ] Unit/integration tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this feature area
- [ ] E2E scripts validate user-facing workflows; logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Performance/UX budgets or evidence updated where applicable; regressions are detectable
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

-{"id":"bd-15jg","title":"Generate test functions from validated manifest (macro-based)","description":"# Generate test functions from validated manifest (macro-based)

## Context
We want each extension to be its own #[test] function for:
- Parallelism (cargo test runs tests in parallel)
- Isolation (one failure does not block others)
- Clear reporting (each extension shows as pass/fail in test output)

## Approach
Similar to fixture_test! macro in conformance_fixtures.rs:

macro_rules! conformance_test {
    ($name:ident, $ext_id:literal) => {
        #[test]
        fn $name() {
            let result = run_conformance_test($ext_id);
            assert!(result.status == "pass", "Extension {} failed: {:?}", $ext_id, result.diffs);
        }
    };
}

conformance_test!(conformance_hello, "hello");
conformance_test!(conformance_pirate, "pirate");
// ... generated for all validated extensions

## Build Script Alternative
Could use a build.rs that reads VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json and generates test functions. This avoids manually maintaining the list. Tradeoff: build.rs adds complexity but eliminates manual maintenance.

## Decision
Start with manual macro invocations for the first 60 official extensions. Add build.rs generation later when corpus is larger.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each official pi-mono extension has a #[test] function
- Tests can be filtered: cargo test conformance_hello
- Tests can be run by tier: cargo test conformance_tier1","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:21:45.866739251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:59:22.299948047Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T00:59:22.299823645Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15jg","depends_on_id":"bd-1no3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:21:45.876810519Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":450,"issue_id":"bd-15jg","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Un-ignored 103 conformance tests that now pass. Tests went from 65 non-ignored to 172 passing (38 still ignored due to actual failures, mostly npm/multi-file deps). All quality gates pass: fmt, check, clippy, test.","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:41:17Z"},{"id":461,"issue_id":"bd-15jg","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fixed 14 manifest mismatches in VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json (command/tool registration discrepancies). Un-ignored 14 more tests. Now at 195 passing, 24 ignored (remaining are load errors from missing npm deps, missing files, or unsupported module specifiers). Commit: 637b21ac","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:59:13Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-15n","title":"Update --continue flag to use SQLite index","description":"# Update --continue flag to use SQLite index

## Goal
Use SQLite index for fast lookup of most recent session when --continue is used.

## Current Implementation (src/main.rs)
Currently scans filesystem for most recent:
```rust
if args.continue_session {
    let session_dir = sessions_dir_for_cwd(&cwd);
    // Scans all files, sorts by mtime
    let most_recent = fs::read_dir(&session_dir)?
        .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
        .max_by_key(|e| e.metadata().ok()?.modified().ok()?);
    // ...
}
```

## New Implementation
```rust
if args.continue_session {
    let index = SessionIndex::open_default()?;
    let cwd = std::env::current_dir()?.to_string_lossy().to_string();
    
    if let Some(meta) = index.find_recent(&cwd)? {
        return load_session(&meta.path);
    }
    
    // Fallback to filesystem
    // ...
}
```

## Benefits
- O(1) lookup instead of O(n) filesystem scan
- Works across hundreds of sessions instantly
- Consistent with session picker behavior

## Dependencies
- bd-3nz (indexing must work first)

## Testing
- Test: --continue uses index
- Test: --continue falls back to filesystem
- Test: Performance improved

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --continue queries index
- [ ] Fallback works if index empty
- [ ] Faster for users with many sessions","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:59.405457245Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:28:18.410171732Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:35:58.232575986Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15n","depends_on_id":"bd-346","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-15n","depends_on_id":"bd-3nz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-167l","title":"Bench: JSONL schema + env fingerprint for extension benchmarks","description":"# Goal
Define the canonical, machine-readable output format for extension benchmark runs.

# Scope / Deliverables
- JSONL record types (per run, per extension, per scenario)
- Environment fingerprint fields:
  - OS/kernel
  - CPU model + core count
  - build profile (debug/release)
  - git commit hash
  - feature flags (ext-conformance, wasm, etc.)
- Timing stats fields:
  - raw samples OR a histogram
  - p50/p95/p99
  - warmup count
- Deterministic formatting + stable key ordering.

# Why
Everything downstream (CI gates, trend tracking, baseline comparisons, docs) depends on this being stable.

# Acceptance
- One end-to-end run emits valid JSONL that validates against the schema.
- Stable ordering across repeated deterministic runs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:01:47.176631505Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:01:47.330115315Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bench","extensions","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-167l","depends_on_id":"bd-20s9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:01:47.330087273Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1696","title":"Create standard event payload fixtures for both runtimes","description":"# Create standard event payload fixtures for both runtimes

## Context
Extensions subscribe to events via pi.on("event_name", handler). For conformance testing, we need to FIRE the same events in both runtimes and compare handler responses.

## Events Defined in pi-mono types.ts
From the ExtensionEvent type union:
- tool_call: {tool, input} -> {allow, message, replace_input}
- tool_result: {tool, result} -> {result} (can modify)
- turn_start: {} -> void
- turn_end: {} -> void
- before_agent_start: {} -> {message, system_prompt}
- input: {content, source} -> {content, skip}
- context: {messages, usage} -> {system_prompt, messages}
- resources_discover: {resources} -> {resources}
- user_bash: {command, result} -> {modified_result}
- session_before_compact: {preparation} -> {should_compact, custom_messages}
- session_before_tree: {options} -> {should_show_tree}

## What To Do
1. For each event type, create 2-3 standard payloads (normal case + edge cases)
2. Save as tests/ext_conformance/fixtures/event_payloads.json
3. Document expected behavior for each event type
4. Include payloads that test: normal flow, blocking behavior, error handling, empty handlers

## Fixture Format
{
  "event_payloads": {
    "tool_call": [
      {
        "name": "basic_tool_call",
        "payload": {"tool": "bash", "input": {"command": "echo hi"}},
        "default_response": {"allow": true}
      },
      {
        "name": "blocked_tool_call",
        "payload": {"tool": "rm", "input": {"path": "/etc/passwd"}},
        "default_response": {"allow": false, "message": "blocked"}
      }
    ]
  }
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- All event types from ExtensionEvent have at least 2 payloads
- Payloads are valid JSON matching the TypeScript type definitions
- Edge cases included (empty arrays, null values, unicode, large payloads)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:34.169239280Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:02:20.870081762Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:02:20.870011520Z","close_reason":"Added missing event types (model_select, session_start/switch/fork/compact/tree) and ensured 2+ payloads each; added second session_shutdown payload","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1696","depends_on_id":"bd-6koq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:34.185490611Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-tyql","title":"Task: Implement input event dispatch","description":"# Task: Implement Input Event Dispatch

## Objective

Dispatch the input event before processing user messages, allowing extensions to transform or block input.

## Background

The input event fires when the user submits a message. Extensions can:
- Transform input (e.g., expand macros, translate)
- Block inappropriate input
- Add attachments
- Log user activity

## TypeScript Reference

```typescript
// Before processing user input
if (runner.hasHandlers('input')) {
    const inputResult = await runner.emit({
        type: 'input',
        content: userMessage.content,
        attachments: userMessage.attachments,
    });
    
    if (inputResult?.block) {
        // Show message and don't process
        console.log(inputResult.reason || 'Input blocked');
        return;
    }
    
    // Use transformed content if provided
    if (inputResult?.content) {
        userMessage.content = inputResult.content;
    }
}
```

## Implementation

### In Interactive Input Handler

```rust
impl Agent {
    async fn process_user_input(
        &mut self,
        content: String,
        attachments: Vec<Attachment>,
        event_dispatcher: Option<&EventDispatcher>,
    ) -> Result<Option<UserMessage>> {
        // Dispatch input event
        if let Some(dispatcher) = event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("input") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::Input {
                    content: content.clone(),
                    attachments: attachments.clone(),
                };
                
                if let Some(result) = dispatcher.dispatch::<InputEventResult>(event).await? {
                    // Check for blocking
                    if result.block {
                        let reason = result.reason.unwrap_or_else(||
                            "Input blocked by extension".to_string()
                        );
                        self.display_message(&reason)?;
                        return Ok(None);
                    }
                    
                    // Use transformed content if provided
                    let final_content = result.content.unwrap_or(content);
                    
                    return Ok(Some(UserMessage {
                        content: final_content,
                        attachments,
                    }));
                }
            }
        }
        
        Ok(Some(UserMessage { content, attachments }))
    }
}
```

## Event Data

### input
```json
{
    "type": "input",
    "content": "What is the weather?",
    "attachments": []
}
```

### With attachments
```json
{
    "type": "input",
    "content": "Analyze this file",
    "attachments": [
        { "type": "file", "path": "/tmp/data.csv" }
    ]
}
```

## Event Result

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Deserialize)]
pub struct InputEventResult {
    /// Transformed content (if None, use original)
    pub content: Option<String>,
    
    /// If true, block input processing
    #[serde(default)]
    pub block: bool,
    
    /// Reason for blocking (shown to user)
    pub reason: Option<String>,
}
```

## Handler Examples

```javascript
// Macro expansion
ctx.on('input', (event) => {
    let content = event.content;
    
    // Expand \!date macro
    if (content.includes('\!date')) {
        content = content.replace('\!date', new Date().toISOString());
        return { content };
    }
    
    return null; // No modification
});

// Content filter
ctx.on('input', (event) => {
    if (containsPII(event.content)) {
        return { 
            block: true, 
            reason: 'Please remove personal information before submitting.' 
        };
    }
    return null;
});
```

## Testing

1. Unit test: No handlers → original input used
2. Unit test: Handler returns null → original input used
3. Unit test: Handler transforms content → transformed used
4. Unit test: Handler blocks → input not processed
5. Unit test: Block reason shown to user
6. Integration test: Full input flow

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-tg4w (EventDispatcher)
- Part of: bd-10vp (Extension Event Hook Dispatch feature)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] input event dispatched before processing
- [ ] Transformation applied correctly
- [ ] Blocking works
- [ ] Block reason displayed
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:12:30.000162514Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:11:33.826257827Z","deleted_at":"2026-02-04T21:11:33.826250723Z","deleted_by":"ubuntu","delete_reason":"Merged into bd-s1hx","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-u7t7","title":"Publish asupersync crate (remove path deps; crates.io readiness + workflow)","description":"# Scope
Repo: `../asupersync`
Crate: `asupersync`

# Key Work
Publishing `asupersync` requires eliminating `path =` dependencies (crates.io rejects them). Today the crate uses local paths for:
- `asupersync-macros` (optional `proc-macros` feature)
- `asupersync-conformance` (optional `cli` feature + dev-dependency)

# Steps
1. Convert local path deps to version deps:
   - Publish `asupersync-macros` first.
   - Publish `asupersync-conformance` first.
   - Replace `path = ...` with `version = ...` in `asupersync/Cargo.toml`.

2. Validate packaging:
   - `cargo package -p asupersync`
   - `cargo publish -p asupersync --dry-run`

3. Release workflow:
   - Add/verify tag-triggered workflow for publishing.

# Acceptance
- `cargo publish -p asupersync --dry-run` succeeds with no path deps.
- Release workflow exists and is safe (tag gated, token gated).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:27:57.371847995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:43:01.734341704Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T00:43:01.734270552Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","crates"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u7t7","depends_on_id":"bd-3247","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:31:47.250103601Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-u7t7","depends_on_id":"bd-5obv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:31:20.417325553Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-u7t7","depends_on_id":"bd-cccv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:27:57.392648760Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":449,"issue_id":"bd-u7t7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed root asupersync publish-readiness update.

Changes made in `../asupersync/Cargo.toml`
- Removed local `path` from optional proc-macro dependency:
  - `asupersync-macros = { version = "0.1.0", optional = true }`
- Removed local `path` from optional conformance dependency:
  - `conformance = { package = "asupersync-conformance", version = "0.1.0", optional = true }`
- Removed local `path` from dev-dependency on conformance:
  - `conformance = { package = "asupersync-conformance", version = "0.1.0" }`

Validation evidence
- `cd /data/projects/asupersync && cargo package --locked --allow-dirty` ✅
- `cd /data/projects/asupersync && cargo publish --dry-run --locked --allow-dirty` ✅
- Publish workflow verification: `.github/workflows/publish.yml` is tag-gated and token-gated (`CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN`) and publishes `asupersync-macros`, `asupersync-conformance`, then `asupersync`.

Notes
- Large warning list about excluded examples/tests during package is expected due package `include` policy and does not block publish.
- Upstream prerequisite beads `bd-5obv` and `bd-3247` were completed first.","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:40:57Z"}]}
